title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Release Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working layered Docker incident evidence packet for logs, events, metrics, inspect, debugging, and incident evidence, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered Docker incident evidence packet while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop container exits only after revision 4; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation, keep Docker Logs Drivers Rotation connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop container exits only after revision 4; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Docker Logs Drivers Rotation condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Docker Engine Container Runtime and Resource Documentation — Controls process lifecycle, PID 1, signals, restart policies, health, CPU, memory, PID, device, logging, events, metrics, and diagnostic evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Logs Drivers Rotation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop container exits only after revision 4; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Logs Drivers Rotation, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: docker logs always reads every application log regardless of the configured logging driver.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Logs Drivers Rotation for layered Docker incident evidence packet.
State the Docker Logs Drivers Rotation preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
docker logs always reads every application log regardless of the configured logging driver. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Docker Logs Drivers Rotation.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Layered Incident Method
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-layered-incident-method-1om2fp". Tailor its incident-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Layered Incident Method.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Layered Incident Method into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Layered Incident Method, keep Docker Layered Incident Method connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-layered-incident-method-1om2fp
  - competency: docker-layered-incident-method
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: layered incident method
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: remote context crossing a separately authorized daemon boundary
    fault-injection: remove dependency readiness while preserving start order
    retained-proof: cold-build trace and content-store usage comparison
    case-key: 1om2fp
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
    failure: Reject interactive mutation as diagnosis, unbounded log collection, high-cardinality labels, or a restart presented as root-cause evidence.
    repair: Preserve state and timestamps, compare desired with observed configuration, isolate one layer, reproduce cleanly, and retain the causal regression.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Layered Incident Method structural check is only one gate. layered Docker incident evidence packet must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
